_doc: Canned responses extracted from the original investigation's raw/ CLI outputs (scrubbed). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Recorded verbatim. The session captured only the Error-level log query; the single row is the job's one Error entry.
No unfiltered-logs raw was recorded; the Error-level set answers any logs variant — the exception row is the only diagnostic content.
Recorded verbatim: Pending -> Running -> Faulted in ~8s, single clean execution, no retries.

Faithful replay of the recorded evidence gap: in the original session trace retrieval FAILED with 'Error retrieving trace ID for job' (exit 1). Every trace-retrieval form returns the same recorded failure so the eval agent faces the same gap — the diagnosis must come from jobs get + error logs.
The form actually run in the original session; the recorded response is the failure payload above, exit code 1.

TraceId-positional form (32-hex from the job record). Not attempted in the original session, but trace retrieval failed there and no span raw exists; serving the same recorded failure preserves the evidence gap instead of fabricating an empty span set via unmocked_default.

No --state Faulted raw was recorded in this session, so there is no Faulted-only fixture; substring dispatch routes `--state Faulted` variants here too, returning the full recorded list — the failing job is the most recent row.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The tenant has one folder of interest (Shared); the recorded folder-scoped job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant the agent tries.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by `rules` returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.

Agent must fetch the failing job: the Info field carries the full Microsoft.Graph.ServiceException — Code: invalidRequest, 'The payload of the request was too large', Inner error Code: maxFileSizeExceeded — with the stack through LargeFileUploadTask.UploadAsync / UploadSliceRequest.PutAsync into legacy UploadFile.ExecuteAsync. This plus the error log is the entire evidence base (traces unavailable).
OPTIONAL — Error-level log (1 entry) repeats the exception already visible in jobs get, anchored to activity 'Upload File' in O365_UploadQuotaSize.xaml.
OPTIONAL — state transitions (Pending -> Running -> Faulted, ~8s) confirm a clean single execution with no retries.
OPTIONAL — every trace-retrieval form replays the recorded failure 'Error retrieving trace ID for job' (exit 1). Traces were unavailable in the real session; no span evidence exists for this job.
